name: SoftBank Group Corp.
reason_label: Activist or corporate-action catalyst
genuine_research_en:
**What happened:** SoftBank Group (SBG) is Japan's largest investment holding company, led by Masayoshi Son — they invest at massive scale in technology companies worldwide: chip-designer Arm Holdings, OpenAI in the US, PayPay, and many Southeast-Asia and India startups. FY3/2026 results were extraordinary — revenue +7.7% to ¥7.80 trillion, and **consolidated net profit jumped 4.3x YoY to ¥5.02 trillion** (+333.7%) — a 5-year-high record-breaker. **Why:** The main reason net profit quadrupled was **AI-related investments materializing as massive valuation gains**. In particular, SBG's large stake in US-based OpenAI was marked up on the books as OpenAI's company valuation surged. Arm is also doing well — royalty revenue is growing on both higher unit pricing and shipping volumes, driven by adoption of cutting-edge tech and AI-server data-center demand. PayPay is also expanding payments and financial balances, boosting fees and interest income. **Stock reaction:** The stock surged sharply around earnings — **+11.9% on the earnings day**, and in one week where the Nikkei 225 rose ¥1,930, **SBG alone contributed ¥814 to that — about 42% of the entire index move**. The market is increasingly viewing SBG as a 'must-own AI name,' and multiple brokerages have reiterated Bullish ratings and raised price targets.

Several structural reasons explain why business AND stock are rising together.

First, symbolic status as an AI-stock proxy. With the OpenAI investment, SBG has become the clearest 'AI exposure (investment opportunity)' available in the Japanese market. For domestic institutional and retail investors who can't easily directly buy NVIDIA or Microsoft in the US, SBG is 'the AI stock you can buy in yen' — a powerful draw.

Second, Arm as a growth engine. Arm's architecture is used in chips from smartphones to AI servers, so it directly benefits from rising AI demand. This is the concrete business foundation underneath SBG's 'AI story.'

Third, capital-efficiency improvement. ROE (return on equity) is trending up, which tends to drive PBR (price-to-book ratio) re-rating.

Fourth, influence on the Nikkei 225. SBG is a high-priced constituent of the index, so it has outsized influence on the index level. When index-tracking ETFs see inflows, a lot of money flows into SBG mechanically, creating supply-demand-driven upward pressure.

The risk: SBG's profit swings dramatically with valuation movements of investee companies (especially OpenAI). The same mechanism that inflated profits via valuation gains works in reverse — if AI-related stocks correct, valuation losses would compress profits sharply.

**[Single-pass combined-prompt verification (2026-06)]** **Revenue ¥7.7987 trillion (+7.7%), net profit ¥5.0023 trillion (+333.7%)** confirmed. OpenAI valuation gain ¥2.1567 trillion is the driver. Growth axes: (1) Arm royalty expansion (AI datacenter demand), (2) PayPay listed but stays a consolidated subsidiary, lifting fee+interest income, (3) OpenAI stake 11% (incl subscription); April 2025 reports of exploring ~$10B loan collateralized by OpenAI shares. ROE/ROA above-benchmark and improving. Risks: rising interest-bearing debt; earnings swings from OpenAI valuation volatility.

第一にAI関連銘柄としての象徴的地位。SBGはOpenAIへの大規模投資により、日本市場で『AIへのエクスポージャー(投資機会)』を求める投資家にとって最も明確な選択肢になっている。米国のNVIDIAやMicrosoftに直接投資しにくい日本の機関投資家・個人投資家にとって、SBGは『日本円で買えるAI銘柄』として強い魅力。

第四に日経平均への影響力。SBGは日経平均株価の構成銘柄の中でも値嵩株(株価が高い銘柄)で、指数全体への影響力が大きい。指数連動型ETFの資金流入はSBGに大きく流れるため、需給面でも上昇しやすい構造。

genuine_research_en:
**What happened:** SKY Perfect JSAT Holdings is Asia's largest satellite operator, with two pillars: 'Space business' (satellite communications/broadcasting, satellite imaging, defense-related space intelligence) and 'Media business' (SKY PerfecTV! pay-TV, Wowow, etc.). FY3/2026 revenue ¥127.58 billion (+3.1%), **operating profit ¥35.27 billion (+28.3%) — a record-high OP**. Net profit ¥23.31 billion (+22.0%). Beat the revised guidance. **Why:** Profit growth was driven by 'Space business growth' and 'Media business cost-cutting effects.' Space revenue ¥69.8 billion (+7.9%), segment profit ¥16.1 billion (+5.6%) — domestic satellite-comm demand, plus **the Ministry of Defense satellite constellation order** (constellation = many small satellites operating in coordination) and growth in Space Intelligence (satellite imagery analysis). Media revenue ¥64.3 billion (-1.9%) — slightly down, but segment profit ¥7.7 billion (**+74.3%**) — distribution competition is squeezing revenue, but fixed-cost compression and efficiency drove margins sharply higher. **Stock reaction:** Stock rose **+30.7% from ¥2,873 (3-Mar) to ¥3,755 (2-Jun-2026)**. On annual basis the rise is larger (over +50%).

Structural drivers behind the stock-up:

First, **re-rating on defense / national-security themes**. With the Japanese government targeting defense spending of 2% of GDP (by FY27), defense-related stocks broadly re-rated; SKY Perfect JSAT — a rare private-sector company with **a concrete government-related Defense Ministry satellite-constellation contract** — drew attention. Worsening security environment is a long-term tailwind.

Second, **record-high earnings + beating guidance**. Beating the revised guidance, 3 consecutive years of OP growth, and record OP / net profit simultaneously — a 'clean' good-earnings story that institutional investors easily digest.

Third, **strong FY27 guidance**. FY3/2027 guidance: revenue +5.8% (¥135 billion), **operating profit +10.6% (¥39 billion), net profit +15.8% (¥27 billion)** — sustained double-digit profit growth outlook.

Fourth, **dividend policy and shareholder returns**. Dividend yield basis appreciated by income-oriented institutional investors.

Fifth, **high-growth Space Intelligence business**. Satellite imagery analytics, IoT data linkage and other new areas are viewed by the market as 'Japan's Maxar (the US satellite-imagery company)' with growth optionality.

Risks: (a) delays in defense-related orders or government-budget volatility, (b) Media business structural decline outpacing cost cuts, (c) satellite-launch failures or other operational risks.

name: TV TOKYO Holdings Corporation

genuine_research_en:
**What happened:** TV Tokyo Holdings is a content-oriented major media group centered on terrestrial broadcasting (TV Tokyo), with **anime / distribution business (including overseas), content sales, BS TV Tokyo (satellite), publishing, and licensing**. FY3/2026 revenue ¥164.92 billion (+5.8%), **operating profit ¥11.40 billion (+46.4%) — a record-high OP**. **Why:** Profit growth was driven by '**overseas-revenue expansion in anime/streaming business**.' Unlike other key broadcasters, TV Tokyo's business model is built on **anime and content sales** rather than news/variety ('Naruto,' 'Boruto,' 'Pokémon,' 'Demon Slayer' (co-production), 'Spy x Family,' 'Solitary Gourmet' etc.) — overseas distribution license sales and IP licensing drove profit. Anime demand in North America and East Asia is rising, with double-digit growth in distribution-rights fees from global platforms like Crunchyroll, Netflix, Prime Video. **Stock reaction:** TV Tokyo HD stock rose +41.8% in 2025, driven by '**profit expansion as a content company**' and '**re-rating from undervalued levels**' together.

Structural stock-up drivers:

First, **long-term cash-flow value of anime IP**. Long-lived IPs like 'Naruto' and 'Pokémon' generate royalties over decade-plus horizons — stock-type revenue. The market is shifting evaluation from 'broadcaster' toward 'content-rights holder' (a category where higher PERs are accepted).

Second, **rise of overseas streaming platforms**. Distribution-rights fees paid by Crunchyroll, Netflix, Prime Video, Disney+ for anime content are rising year-over-year — TV Tokyo is in a 'seller's market.' Rising overseas-revenue ratio also hedges FX risk.

Third, **dividend-hike policy and PBR improvement**. TV Tokyo HD is also responding to the TSE PBR-improvement request — lifting payout ratio and conducting buybacks. Yield improvement attracted both retail and institutional buying.

Fourth, **differentiated business mix vs other key broadcasters**. NTV / TBS / TV Asahi / Fuji are highly dependent on terrestrial ad revenue, while TV Tokyo has a higher content/licensing-revenue ratio — making earnings less swingy with ad-market cycles. This earned a 'stable-growth' premium.

Fifth, **'Solitary Gourmet' global expansion**. Live-action drama is also gaining international popularity, broadening the content portfolio.

Risks: (a) drought in new anime IP hits, (b) downward pressure on platform license fees (platform-side bargaining power rising), (c) production-cost inflation (voice actor / studio rate increases) compressing margins.

**[Single-pass combined-prompt verification (2026-06)]** **Revenue ¥164.92 billion (+5.8%), OP ¥11.40 billion (+46.4%), NP ¥7.7 billion (+27.61%)** — record. **Equity ratio 68.9%**. Growth engine 'Anime/Distribution' — NARUTO/BORUTO overseas game royalty income drove segment profit 1.5x. Broadcasting CPM raises also helped. Mid-term plan: CaaS (Content-as-a-Service) strategy + global IP-media transformation. FY28 guidance: revenue ¥165 billion, OP ¥11.5 billion. PER 15.7x forecast, PBR 1.12x, yield 2.21%.

第五に**『孤独のグルメ』のグローバル展開**。実写ドラマでも海外人気が広がっており、コンテンツの幅が広がっている。

リスクは、(a)アニメIPの新作ヒット枯渇、(b)海外配信プラットフォームの権料引き下げ圧力(プラットフォーム側の交渉力上昇)、(c)制作費インフレ(声優・スタジオ単価上昇)による利益率圧迫。

name: TOHO CO.,LTD
reason_label: Sector-specific tailwind — content/IP/defense category re-rating
genuine_research_en:
**What happened:** Toho is Japan's largest film production, distribution, and exhibition company, known for distributing major hits like 'Godzilla,' 'Shin Godzilla,' 'Your Name,' and the Demon Slayer movies. Beyond film, they run real estate (including the TOHO Cinemas chain and building rentals), theater (TOHO Musicals), and an anime business. FY2/2026 results were strong — **operating revenue ¥360.6 billion (+15.2%), operating profit ¥67.9 billion (+5.0%)** — both up. **Why:** The biggest contribution came from two megahits: the latest Demon Slayer film and 'Kokuhō.' The film business was strong, and the IP/Anime business also grew. The structure works like this: when one of Toho's accumulated hit-chain characters/IPs lands a megahit, related merchandise, licensing revenue, and streaming-rights revenue all rise alongside. **Stock reaction:** This is a textbook case where fundamentals improvement and stock price move in lockstep upward.

Several structural reasons explain why business and stock are rising together.

First, **fortress balance sheet**. Toho's equity ratio is well above the 30% considered healthy, and interest-bearing debt is trending down. Insolvency risk is essentially zero, making it an easy-to-hold defensive name for institutional investors.

Second, **profitability improvement**. Net margin has improved YoY across many quarters, and ROE/ROA meet conventional targets — profitability is stable. Quarter-to-quarter the film-hit calendar creates volatility, but the long-term trend is up.

Third, **stable real-estate revenue base**. In addition to running cinemas, Toho owns substantial real estate in the Tokyo metropolitan area (the TOHO building, etc.), with rental income providing a stable base. This supports profit even in film-business weak periods.

Fourth, **adaptation to the streaming era**. Licensing to Netflix and Amazon Prime Video, overseas distribution, and merchandise expansion — Toho is expanding revenue streams to fit the digital age. With Demon Slayer as the model, the 'film → anime → merchandise → live event' IP-extension monetization is now established.

Fifth, **Toho brand reliability**. As the established film-industry incumbent, the Toho brand provides negotiation power in the content industry — getting source-IP rights, securing streaming deals, etc. That's a powerful intangible asset.

This is an orthodox case of fundamentals driving stock — heavy institutional long-term holding demand.

**何が起きたか：** Toho(東宝)は日本最大の映画制作・配給・興行会社で、『ゴジラ』『シン・ゴジラ』『君の名は。』『鬼滅の刃 劇場版』などの大型ヒット作の配給で知られる。映画事業に加えて、不動産事業(東宝シネマズ含む映画館運営とビル賃貸)、演劇事業(東宝ミュージカル)、アニメ事業も展開している。2026年2月期決算は素晴らしい結果 — **営業収入3,606億円(+15.2%)、営業利益678.9億円(+5.0%)と増収増益**を達成した。 **理由：** 最大の貢献は『鬼滅の刃』と『国宝』という2つの大ヒット作。映画事業が好調に推移し、加えてIP・アニメ事業も伸長した。東宝が長年積み上げてきたヒット作チェーン(キャラクター・IP)のうち、特定の作品が大ヒットすると、関連グッズ・ライセンス収入・配信権収入なども連動して伸びる構造。 **株価の反応：** 業績の改善と株価が連動して上昇している教科書的な銘柄。

業績と株価が共に上昇する構造的な理由はいくつかある。

第一に**鉄壁の財務基盤**。東宝の自己資本比率は一般的に望ましいとされる30%を大きく上回る高水準を維持し、有利子負債は減少傾向。倒産リスクが極めて低く、機関投資家のディフェンシブ銘柄として安心して保有できる。

第二に**収益性の改善**。純利益率は前年同期比で多くの四半期で改善し、ROEとROAも目安を満たし収益性が安定。映画ヒット作の有無で短期変動はあるが、長期トレンドは右肩上がり。

第三に**不動産事業の安定収益**。映画館運営に加え、首都圏に多数の不動産(東宝ビル等)を保有しており、賃貸収入が安定したベース収益を生む。映画事業の不調期でも下支えする構造。

第四に**アニメ・配信時代への対応**。Netflix、Amazon Prime Video等への配信ライセンス、海外配給、グッズ展開など、デジタル時代に合わせた収益源を拡大している。『鬼滅の刃』のように、映画→アニメ→グッズ→ライブイベントというIP横展開で稼ぐ構造が確立されつつある。

第五に**東宝ブランドの信頼性**。映画業界の老舗大手としてのブランドは、コンテンツ業界での交渉力(原作の獲得、配信権の確保等)を支える強力な無形資産。

業績の改善が株価に連動する正統派の銘柄で、機関投資家からの長期保有需要が厚い。

**【単一検索検証(2026-06)で確認された事項】** **売上¥3,606.63億円(+15.2%)、営業¥678.89億円(+5.0%)、経常¥701.40億円(+8.8%)、純利益¥517.69億円(+19.4%)で過去最高**。映画事業: 営業収入¥1,826億円(+30.6%)、営業利益¥373億円(+30.3%)。映画興行入場者¥4,900万人(+27.6%)、配給収入¥1,399億円(歴代最高)。**主要ヒット作**: 劇場版『鬼滅の刃』無限城編第一章 + 国宝(2025年No.1 興収¥56億円)+ 名探偵コナン + チェンソーマン。**戦略的取組**: ワーナー・ブラザース日本配給契約(2026年開始)、¥50億円TOHO-ONE顧客データ基盤、¥130億円自己株取得(750万株)。アナリスト平均目標¥1,864円(強気買い4、買い1、中立4)。IP・アニメ事業利益2032年に対FY25比+200%目標。

name: CAPCOM CO.,LTD.
reason_label: Sustained compounder
genuine_research_en:
**What happened:** Capcom is a Japanese game company holding many globally famous IPs (intellectual property): Monster Hunter, Resident Evil (バイオハザード), Street Fighter, Devil May Cry, and more. They operate across console games, PC games, mobile, arcade, merchandise, esports, and film adaptations. Q3 cumulative results were excellent — revenue ¥115.3 billion (+29.8%), operating profit ¥54.3 billion (+75.1%) — strong top and bottom growth. The flagship Digital Contents business (game software sales) and the Amusement business (arcade equipment and venue operation) both recovered, contributing to the result. **Why:** Capcom's biggest strength is the 'catalog sales' model — past hit titles (Monster Hunter, Resident Evil, etc.) keep selling via digital download for years after release, with revenue spikes tied to new-title launches. This stretches the revenue peak of each new title and creates a structurally stable profit base. They also distribute across multiple platforms (Nintendo Switch, PS5, Steam, etc.), so they're diversified by geography and platform too. **Stock reaction:** As of the May 13, 2026 full-year results, the stock was at ¥3,150 with forward PER 22.7x and PBR 4.93x. **Forward ROE 21.7% and forward ROA 17.09%** show very high profitability; market cap is ¥1.65 trillion. The market values Capcom's durable growth story highly.

Several structural reasons explain why business and stock are rising together.

First, overwhelming IP strength. The Monster Hunter series is consistently a blockbuster in Japan, and each new release also boosts sales of prior entries. Resident Evil retains massive global popularity, and IP-licensing revenue from films, TV series, and theme parks is also expanding.

Second, the quality of profitability. ROE above 21% is exceptional for a game company — it means stable catalog sales combined with new launches produce structurally high margins. Investors deeply value 'high margin quality.'

Third, 12-quarter improvement trend. Performance has been consistently improving, with revenue expansion and stable profitability coexisting. That makes Capcom an 'easy-to-forecast name' — ideal for institutional portfolios.

Fourth, investor psychology. Investors cite 'fundamentals 67% / technicals 33%' as drivers of upside expectations — meaning the rally is recognized as fundamentals-driven, which is the healthiest kind.

Fifth, AI-theme tailwind. Capcom is researching AI applications in game development, adding another 'AI exposure' angle.

This is a textbook case of fundamentals-and-stock virtuous cycle. Institutional long-term holding demand is heavy.

**[Single-pass combined-prompt verification (2026-06)]** **Revenue ¥195.3 billion (+15.2%), OP ¥75.2 billion (+14.5%), NP ¥54.5 billion (+12.7%) — 11 consecutive years of profit growth**. Digital sales ratio ~83%, OP margin 38-39%. Repeat-title share: 50% (2015) → over 70% (2023). Overseas revenue ~80%, 230+ countries. OP 5x over 10 years. **Monster Hunter Wilds: 8 million units in 3 days**. Jeffries maintains Buy. Market expects 10% growth, company tracking 24% — PER ~30x. Caveat: Wilds sales below expectations triggered drop from ¥4,800 ATH to ¥4,000 range.
genuine_research_jp:
**何が起きたか：** Capcom(カプコン)は日本のゲーム会社で、世界的に有名な大型IP(知的財産)を多数保有 — 『モンスターハンター』『バイオハザード』『ストリートファイター』『デビルメイクライ』など。家庭用ゲーム、PCゲーム、モバイル、アーケード、グッズ販売、eスポーツ、映画化など多角的に事業展開している。第3四半期累計の決算は素晴らしい — 売上高1,153億円(+29.8%)、営業利益543億円(+75.1%)と大幅な増収増益。主力のデジタルコンテンツ事業(ゲームソフト販売)とアミューズメント事業(アーケードゲーム機・施設運営)の回復が貢献。 **理由：** Capcomの最大の強みは『カタログ販売』 — 過去にリリースした名作タイトル(モンハン、バイオなど)が、新作リリースと連動してダウンロード販売で何年も売れ続ける収益モデル。これにより新作1本ごとの売上ピークが長期化、安定的に利益を稼ぐ構造が定着した。さらに任天堂Switch、PS5、Steamなど複数プラットフォームで展開しており、地域・プラットフォーム別の分散も効いている。 **株価の反応：** 2026年5月13日の本決算発表時点で株価は3,150円、PER予想22.7倍、PBR実績4.93倍。**ROE予想21.7%、ROA予想17.09%**と非常に高い収益性を示しており、時価総額は1兆6,470億円。市場はCapcomの継続的な成長性を高く評価している。

業績と株価が共に上昇する構造的な理由はいくつかある。

第一に圧倒的なIPの強さ。『モンスターハンター』シリーズは日本国内で爆発的なヒットを継続しており、新作のたびに過去作の売上も牽引する。『バイオハザード』シリーズも世界的人気を維持しており、映画・ドラマ・テーマパーク展開などのIPライセンス収入も拡大している。

第二に収益性の質。ゲーム会社の中でROE 21%超は際立って高い水準で、これは安定したカタログ販売と新作の組み合わせで利益率が構造的に高いことを意味する。投資家は『利益率の高さ』を高く評価する。

第三に過去12四半期の安定改善トレンド。業績は継続的に改善傾向で、売上の拡大基調と収益性の安定が両立している。これは投資家にとって『予測しやすい銘柄』であり、機関投資家のポートフォリオに入れやすい。

第四に投資家心理。投資家の上昇予想理由は『業績67%・テクニカル33%』と、業績改善を主要な上昇要因として認識されている — つまり業績ドリブンの健全な上昇。

第五にAI関連テーマでの追い風。Capcomはゲーム制作にAIを活用する研究を進めており、AI関連銘柄としての注目も加わっている。

業績と株価が連動する『教科書的な好循環』の銘柄で、機関投資家からの長期保有需要が厚い。

**【単一検索検証(2026-06)で確認された事項】** **売上¥1,953億円(+15.2%)、営業¥752億円(+14.5%)、純利益¥545億円(+12.7%) — 11期連続増益**。デジタル販売比率約83%、営業利益率38-39%。リピート作比率2015年50%→2023年70%超。海外売上比率約80%、世界230以上の国・地域展開。営業利益は10年で5倍。**モンスターハンターワイルズ発売3日間で800万本**。ジェフリーズが買い評価維持。次の3年で年24%成長見込み(市場は10%予想)、PER30倍前後。注意点: Wilds販売想定下振れで株価が¥4,800台ATH→¥4,000台に下落の動き。
